result:
All four artifacts reproduce from a clean state, exit 0, no warnings. Memo is 435 words of argument (~450 target). Done.

## Result

Routed as a **Fable-led orchestration**. I kept the judgment core (spec design, SE methodology, verdict) and wrote/ran the correctness-critical `script.R` myself. The one genuinely high-blast-radius **and** hard-to-verify call — which standard error is defensible, and does matching *recover* the benchmark — went to the guardrail's **parallel blind-peer path**: Opus (deep-reasoner) and Codex (GPT-5.6 Terra), same prompt, blind to each other and to my code, then synthesized on merits.

**The finding.** Benchmark **+$1,794** [479, 3,109]; naive observational **−$8,498**. Across 6 specs (2 covariate sets × 3 estimators), recovery is **favorable-specification-only**:

| Covariate set | 1-NN | 1-NN+trim | Stratify(5) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Demographics | −2,798 | −2,798 | −4,162 |
| + re74/re75 | **+1,712** ✓ | **+1,759** ✓ | −144 |

Only 1-NN matching *with* lagged earnings covers the benchmark. Demographics-only never does; the same rich score through coarse stratification collapses to a null. That joint dependence on covariate set **and** estimator is Smith-Todd's fragility, not Dehejia-Wahba's robust replication.

**Both blind peers independently reached the same verdict** and the same claim/no-claim boundary — high confidence on the adjudication. They diverged on one SE detail, which I resolved on merits: Codex's sharper caution (don't dress a sandwich SE up as the Abadie-Imbens estimator) was correct, so I kept the cluster-robust SE that MatchIt-only permits but **relabeled it honestly** as an approximation to the unavailable AI (2006) variance, with the bootstrap ruled out per AI (2008). No estimate changed — only labeling honesty. Used **2 of 3 delegations**; the revision cycle went to that relabeling.

**Deliverables** (all in the working directory, all reproduce clean):
- `script.R` — self-contained, Okabe-Ito theme + `set.seed(20260713)` up top, per T1/T2 convention
- `figures/spec-curve.png` — 2560×1664 @ 320 dpi, one figure, benchmark as reference line + CI band, no in-plot title
- `spec-table.md` — every spec vs. benchmark with a gap column and a "covers?" flag, plus the naive estimate and the SE note
- `memo.md` — 435-word adjudication: concedes what conditioning on covariates does (closes most of the gap, chiefly via earnings history) and doesn't (certify unconfoundedness), verdict = favorable-specification-only, and the precise line on what a paper may and may not claim

One honest note worth flagging: for the demographics-only model, common-support trimming removed nothing (identical estimates) — overlap only becomes binding once `re74/re75` enter the score. That's a real feature of the data, documented in the script, not a bug.
